Package index

Estimators (lavaan syntax generators)

Functions that emit lavaan model syntax for panel-data models.

estimateCLPM()
estimateCLPM. This follows the Usami, Murayama, & Hamaker (2019) unified framework for longitudinal models.
estimateRICLPM()
estimateRICLPM
estimateRICLPM3()
estimateRICLPM3
estimateRICLPMl()
estimateRICLPMl
estimateRICLPM_nolag()
estimateRICLPM_nolag
estimateRI()
estimateRI
estimateLChange()
estimateLChange
estimateLGM()
estimateLGM
estimateALT()
estimateALT, following Usami, Murayama, & Hamaker (2019) unified frameowk.
estimateLCMSR()
estimateLCMSR. This is just the GCLPM. Structure the means here.
estimateBollen_and_Brand()
estimateBollen_and_Brand
estimateTSO()
estimateTSO
estimateStTr()
estimateStTr
estimateAllisonChamberlainFI()
estimateAllisonChamberlainFI
estimateCLPM_MI()
estimateCLPM_MI
estimateRICLPM_MI()
estimateRICLPM_MI
estimateALT_MI()
estimateALT_MI
estimateLCMSR_MI()
estimateLCMSR_MI
estimateBollen_and_Brand_MI()
estimateBollen_and_Brand_MI

Data simulators

Generate panel data under known parameters.

simCLPM()
simCLPM
simCLPMu()
simCLPMu
simCLPM_timeInvariantU()
simCLPM_timeInvariantU

simLChange()
simLChange
simLGM()
simLGM
simWithinBetween()
simWithinBetween
sim_trait_state()
sim_trait_state
baseRICLPM_sim()
Simulate Data from a Random Intercept Cross-Lagged Panel Model (RI-CLPM)

Monte Carlo runners

Replication wrappers that fit estimators across many simulated datasets.

monteCarloAllisonChamberlainFI()
monteCarloAllisonChamberlainFI
monteCarloCLPM()
monteCarloCLPM
monteCarloCTSEM()
monteCarloCTSEM
monteCarloConfounder()
monteCarloConfounder
monteCarloFixed()
monteCarloFixed
monteCarloLChange()
monteCarloLChange
monteCarloOLS()
monteCarloOLS
monteCarloRI()
monteCarloRI
monteCarloRICLPM()

monteCarloTraitState()
monteCarloTraitState
monteCarlo_OLS()
simpleSim
run_mc_sims()
run_mc_sims

Helpers

Utilities for reshaping, plotting, and continuous-time SEM.

reshape_long_sim_cr()
reshape_long_sim_cr
compCTSEM()
compCTSEM
riclpm_graph()
riclpm_graph
withinBetween()
withinBetween
plotWithinBetween()
plotWithinBetween
zero.one()
Create a zero one normalization
